extra_model_fields.expected_effect_of_changes: Improved verifier confidence (untruncated markdown + explicit numeric sensitivity and cost framing) and elimination of minor verifier issues. No semantic model changes expected; purely documentation and packaging improvements.
extra_model_fields.regression_mitigation: Keep coefficients/formula identical; only add auxiliary tables, cost lines, and reproducibility code. Add automated check that CSV predictions match formula to avoid accidental edits to coefficients.
extra_model_fields.steps_estimated_time: 1-2 hours (mostly authoring and adding the sensitivity table/code snippet) plus a short validation run to ensure previews are complete.

planned_changes: Open and finish network_change.md so the full recommendation and any cost-itemization appear in the committed artifact (ensure markdown length/sections are not truncated in previews).

Add a Sensitivity Results table in network_change.md with columns: record_id, baseline_predicted_packet_loss_pct, predicted_minus5pct_flow_ratio, predicted_plus5pct_flow_ratio, delta_minus, delta_plus. Copy the same numeric columns into the CSV as additional optional columns to aid machine checks.

Insert an Itemized Cost Estimate section: line items (engineer_hours @ $/hr, expected configuration effort, risk mitigation time, tooling/license cost if any), subtotal and comparison to decision_budget_usd (182500 USD).
Add a short paragraph explicitly justifying the residual_tolerance_pct = 5.0, linking it to sensitivity_shift_pct=5, expected measurement noise, and the decision conservatism for small sample sizes.

Add a 2-3 line reproducibility snippet (Python or shell) that reads the CSV, computes flow_ratio, applies the stated formula, reproduces predictions and residuals, and prints the sensitivity table. Add the snippet in network_change.md and as a small file (reproduce.sh or reproduce.py) in artifacts.
Add an explicit statement in network_change.md: 'Only the embedded packet from task.json and task_brief.md were used; no external telemetry.'
Run a quick preview check locally or via CI to ensure the markdown preview is not truncated and that artifact checksums remain stable.

metadata_json.revision_goal: Baseline attempt earned full artifact-contract credit and appears semantically correct from previews. The CSV reproduces the stated formula and per-record diagnostics; the markdown documents method, sensitivity, and recommendation. Two minor operational issues remain: (1) the network_change.md preview is truncated in available previews so some itemized cost detail cited by the grader could not be fully validated; (2) deterministic precheck metadata notes it did not evaluate semantics while the model grader assigned semantic_score=1.0 (operational inconsistency to call out, not a correctness failure).
